description_long: This object is a solid, single-piece shoe last, typically used in shoemaking to mold and shape footwear. It is made from a dense, blue material with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and features a streamlined, anatomically contoured form that mimics the shape of a human foot. The last has a rounded toe, a defined arch, and a raised heel section, with several small holes along the side, likely for ventilation or attachment during manufacturing. There are visible markings and numbers printed on the surface, indicating size and possibly manufacturer details. The top of the heel area has a small circular recess, possibly for hanging or handling. The overall appearance is clean, robust, and utilitarian, with no visible signs of wear or damage.
description: A solid blue shoe last with a smooth, contoured surface, printed markings, and small side holes, used for shaping shoes.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the rounded toe, smooth upper surface, and visible size markings on the side.
description_view_1: Front-right view highlighting the elongated toe, arch, and the gentle slope of the instep.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the heel, side holes, and additional printed information near the back.
description_view_3: Rear-left view focusing on the heel contour, side holes, and the top recess for handling.
